title: paronychia capitata subsp. chlorothyrsa
intro: Paronychia capitata subsp. chlorothyrsa, also known as Mexican tea or narrow-leaved paronychia, is a plant species in the family Amaranthaceae. It is a shrubby perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly in its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Paronychia capitata subsp. chlorothyrsa include its use in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or decoction, and it can also be used in salves and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
